work.name: UChicago Data Science Institute
highlights: Fine-tuned multiple transformer-based large language models for summarization of convoluted and lengthy (1500+ word) legal documents pertaining to the finances of large international banks.
Created multiple bespoke models capable of creating summaries of documents with 90% of relevant information at 10% of the original length.
Explored challenges in AI alignment, analyzing solutions to address deceptive behavior in AI systems.
Investigated security vulnerabilities in large language models, focusing on adversarial examples and techniques such as RLHF to mitigate AI exploitation.

projects.name: UChicago Crime Map
highlights: Utilized Python and R to web scrape, clean, geocode, and map over 1000 UChicago-area crimes from the UChicago Police Department’s website.
Deployed map as a website to serve as a safety resource for the student body (uchicago-crime.github.io/map).

projects.name: Python Implementation of Go
highlights: Developed a playable GO game in a tema of four students, including the game logic, a GUI, a terminal-based interface, an alogithmic game-playing bot, and tests.

projects.name: Custom Shell with File Compressor
highlights: Developed a Unix-based shell supporting built-in commands, I/O redirection, and advanced I/O inserting.
Included a LZW file compressor with pruning to allow for more efficient compression, capable of compressing files up to 50%.
